items.acceptance_criteria: Operator triggers `gitcode-mcp sync --live` through the real CLI entrypoint against a mocked GitCode HTTP API; the CLI uses the live provider startup route, sends requests to the mock server when credentials are usable, and an offline CLI integration test fails if fixture records such as `ISSUE-42` or `WIKI-HOME` appear.
items.actor_trigger: Operator runs sync with `--live` and usable test credentials against a mocked GitCode API.
items.expected_outcome: Live provider is constructed and mock server receives requests; fixture identifiers are absent.
items.freshness: Current source and test binary from the target repository.
items.request_item: Explicit `sync --live` must reach the live provider through the same CLI/startup path an operator uses.

items.acceptance_criteria: Operator triggers `gitcode-mcp sync --live` with no environment token and no mocked credential source; the CLI returns typed missing-credential diagnostics, exits with the expected failure status, leaves the mock server request count at zero, and this is proven by an offline CLI integration test.
items.actor_trigger: Operator runs sync with `--live` and no environment or mocked credential.
items.expected_outcome: Typed missing-credential diagnostic, expected failure status, and zero HTTP requests.
items.freshness: Current target test run with fresh temporary config and cache.
items.request_item: `sync --live` with no usable credential fails with typed missing-credential diagnostics and makes no mock-server requests.

items.acceptance_criteria: Operator triggers `gitcode-mcp create-issue --live` with `GITCODE_TOKEN` unset and a mocked Keychain-equivalent credential source configured for the CLI process; the write credential gate accepts the resolved token, the mock server receives the authenticated create request, and an offline CLI integration test fails if auth status can see the credential but live write cannot.
items.actor_trigger: Operator runs create issue with `GITCODE_TOKEN` unset and a mocked Keychain-equivalent credential source.
items.expected_outcome: Resolved credential is consumed by the live write path and the mock server receives the authenticated request.
items.freshness: Current target runtime test without OS Keychain access.
items.request_item: A mocked credential source equivalent to Keychain can satisfy live write credential checks with `GITCODE_TOKEN` unset.
items.target_surface: Credential resolution and CLI command `gitcode-mcp create-issue --live`

items.acceptance_criteria: Operator triggers `gitcode-mcp sync --live` with an invalid test token against a mock server returning 401 or 403; the CLI reports an auth failure from the live provider, records no fixture success, and the offline CLI integration test proves the mock server received the request.
items.actor_trigger: Operator runs sync with an invalid token against a mock server returning 401 or 403.
items.expected_outcome: CLI reports live auth failure and does not report fixture success.
items.freshness: Current test binary and fresh mock server run.
items.request_item: `sync --live` with an invalid token reaches the mocked live provider and reports 401/403 auth failure, not fixture success.

items.acceptance_criteria: Operator triggers `gitcode-mcp sync --live` with a valid test token against the mocked GitCode API; the CLI fetches mock issues/wiki/comments, writes them into the configured cache, excludes fixture identifiers `ISSUE-42` and `WIKI-HOME`, and an offline CLI integration test verifies cache state through the target app runtime.
items.actor_trigger: Operator runs live sync with a valid test token against sanitized mock issue/wiki/comment endpoints.
items.expected_outcome: Cache contains mock issues, wiki pages, and comments; fixture identifiers are absent.
items.freshness: Fresh temporary cache populated by current target runtime.
items.request_item: `sync --live` with a valid test token populates cache from mocked issue/wiki/comment responses and does not produce fixture records.
items.target_surface: CLI command `gitcode-mcp sync --live` and cache runtime

items.acceptance_criteria: Operator triggers `gitcode-mcp create-issue --live` with a valid test token against the mocked GitCode API; the CLI sends a create request with the expected idempotency behavior, records audit/cache confirmation visible through the target cache or audit surface, never returns `fixture client is read-only`, and an offline CLI integration test verifies the runtime result.
items.actor_trigger: Operator creates an issue with live mode and a valid test token against the mock API.
items.expected_outcome: Create request reaches mock server; audit and cache confirmation are recorded; fixture read-only error is absent.
items.freshness: Current target runtime with fresh temporary audit/cache state.
items.request_item: `create-issue --live` reaches the mocked server, records audit/cache confirmation, and never returns `fixture client is read-only`.
items.target_surface: CLI command `gitcode-mcp create-issue --live`, audit, and cache

items.acceptance_criteria: Operator triggers `gitcode-mcp sync` without `--live` while a mock server is available; the CLI completes through the fixture-backed offline path, the mock server receives zero requests, and a CLI integration test proves default behavior is unchanged.
items.actor_trigger: Operator runs sync without `--live` while a mock server is available.
items.expected_outcome: Offline fixture-backed behavior is used and mock server request count remains zero.
items.freshness: Current target test run with isolated server counter.
items.request_item: Ordinary `sync` without `--live` remains fixture-backed and does not call the mock server.

items.acceptance_criteria: Operator configures the selected API base URL authority and triggers `gitcode-mcp sync --live`; the live HTTP client uses exactly that base URL, ignores non-authoritative alternatives according to the documented rule, and offline CLI integration tests prove request routing by mock server hit counts and failure of the non-selected endpoint.
items.actor_trigger: Operator configures the selected API base URL authority and runs `gitcode-mcp sync --live`.
items.expected_outcome: Live client uses only the selected authority and request routing proves the rule.
items.freshness: Current target tests with separate selected and non-selected mock endpoints.
items.request_item: Resolve and document one explicit API base URL rule for live commands.
items.target_surface: Live HTTP client base URL selection

items.acceptance_criteria: Operator triggers `gitcode-mcp doctor --live --format json`; the CLI returns JSON showing effective live provider mode, credential source, cache path, and API base URL used by the live client, and an offline CLI test verifies the fields using sanitized temporary paths and mocked credentials.
items.actor_trigger: Operator runs doctor in live mode with mocked credentials and configured cache/API base URL.
items.expected_outcome: JSON reports effective live provider mode, credential source, cache path, and API base URL.
items.freshness: Current target runtime and fresh temporary configuration.
items.request_item: `doctor --live` reports effective provider mode, credential source, cache path, and API base URL used by the live client.

items.acceptance_criteria: Developer runs `go test ./...`; offline CLI live integration tests start a mocked GitCode-compatible HTTP server, run real CLI command paths for sync/create/doctor, assert request counts and cache/audit outcomes, and pass without real credentials, external network, or OS Keychain access.
items.expected_outcome: Offline CLI live integration tests pass and prove provider wiring without external dependencies.
items.freshness: Current source tree and test suite.
items.request_item: `go test ./...` passes without real credentials, network, or OS Keychain access.
